canonical.title: Chef-driven & destination tables in and around Ghent (2026)

parent.title: Standout dining in and around Ghent — 2026

siblings.title: Michelin & Gault&Millau-rated tables
siblings.summary: 4 Michelin stars in city limits, a 2-star benchmark 30km away, and where Gault&Millau places everything else.

siblings.title: Romantic & special-occasion settings
siblings.summary: Six Ghent rooms worth a big night out — from a 24-seat Michelin star in a 1671 building to candlelit canal-side warehouses.

siblings.title: Cocktail-led dining & bar-restaurants
siblings.summary: Five Ghent venues where the drinks programme is at least as serious as the food.

siblings.title: Distinctive cuisines beyond Franco-Belgian
siblings.summary: Standout non-Franco-Belgian tables — omakase, ramen, Vietnamese, Thai, Korean, Pakistani, Syrian-Lebanese, Moroccan, Spanish, Turkish.

siblings.title: Remarkable locations & rooms
siblings.summary: Five venues where the room is the reason — a 15th-century butchers' hall, a converted Franciscan church, an 1880s warehouse.

siblings.title: Review & rating sources to mine
siblings.summary: Which review platforms to scrape, ranked by signal density.

sources.title: vrijmoed.be
sources.claim: Vrijmoed is the official site for chef Michaël Vrijmoed's two-star restaurant in Ghent.

sources.title: njam.tv
sources.claim: Michaël Vrijmoed worked as sous-chef at Hof van Cleve for eight years before opening his own restaurant in 2013.

sources.title: guide.michelin.com — OAK
sources.claim: OAK in Ghent holds one Michelin star.

sources.title: visitflanders.com — Ballardin
sources.claim: Marcelo Ballardin has Brazilian-Italian roots; lived in Brazil, US, Italy, England before settling in Belgium.

sources.title: publiekgent.be
sources.claim: Publiek is Olly Ceulenaere's vegetable-forward Michelin-starred bistro on Ham 39 in Ghent.

sources.title: guide.michelin.com — Publiek
sources.claim: Publiek champions little-known vegetables and consciously avoids truffles and caviar.

sources.title: commotiegent.be
sources.claim: Commotie's kitchen revolves around open fire, foraging, fermentation and produce from its own garden.

sources.title: guide.michelin.com — Commotie
sources.claim: Commotie is on Victor Braeckmanlaan in Sint-Amandsberg; chef Thomas Gellynck — nose-to-tail.

sources.title: guide.michelin.com — Hof van Cleve 2★ 2024
sources.claim: Hof van Cleve was reset to two stars under successor chef Floris Van Der Veken in the 2024 Michelin Guide.

sources.title: hofvancleve.com
sources.claim: Hof van Cleve is in Kruisem, East Flanders — roughly 30 km southwest of Ghent.

sources.title: guide.michelin.com — Boury
sources.claim: Boury, Roeselare, holds three Michelin stars.

sources.title: guide.michelin.com — Boury 3★ 2022
sources.claim: Boury rose to three Michelin stars in the 2022 edition of the guide.

sources.title: Wikipedia — Hertog Jan
sources.claim: Hertog Jan was awarded a third Michelin star in 2012 in Bruges, closed at its Bruges/Zedelgem location in December 2018, and reopened at Botanic Sanctuary Antwerp in fall 2021.

sources.title: botanicantwerp.be
sources.claim: Hertog Jan at Botanic, Antwerp, runs an Omakase menu under Gert De Mangeleer; Joachim Boudens is sommelier.

sources.title: guide.michelin.com — Hertog Jan at Botanic
sources.claim: Hertog Jan at Botanic holds two Michelin stars.

sources.title: lepristine.com
sources.claim: Le Pristine is Sergio Herman's New Italian one-Michelin-star restaurant in Antwerp.

sources.title: guide.michelin.com — Le Pristine
sources.claim: Le Pristine, Antwerp, is one Michelin star.

sources.title: bartdepooterstories.com
sources.claim: Pastorale, Bart De Pooter's restaurant in Reet, holds two Michelin stars and a green Michelin star.

sources.title: elianerestaurant.be
sources.claim: Eliane runs a daily ~18-course tasting menu, three-month booking window.

sources.title: guide.michelin.com — Eliane
sources.claim: Eliane (Brussels) has 22 seats including ~half at a bar at the open kitchen counter.

sources.title: gaultmillau.be — Eliane
sources.claim: Eliane was named Gault&Millau Remarkable Newcomer of the Year 2026 for Brussels.

sources.title: vrt.be — Eliane opens
sources.claim: Kobe Desramaults opened Eliane in Brussels in October 2024.

sources.title: vrt.be — Pajottenland pop-up
sources.claim: Desramaults relocates Eliane to a Lennik farmhouse for a July–August 2026 pop-up.

sources.title: debragent.be
sources.claim: Debra is Jason Blanckaert's à la carte fine-dining restaurant at Lange Steenstraat 10 in Ghent.

sources.title: culinaireambiance.com — Debra
sources.claim: Debra opened in 2025 as a return to Blanckaert's J.E.F.-era concept.

sources.title: made-in.be — Lubbi
sources.claim: Sigurdarson and Michiel closed their Brabantdam Souvenir in 2025 and opened bistro Lubbi.

sources.title: Wikipedia — Kobe Desramaults
sources.claim: Desramaults received his first Michelin star at age 25 with In de Wulf; closed Chambre Séparée at end of 2020.

sources.title: foodandwinegazette.com — Chambre Séparée close
sources.claim: Chambre Séparée's closure was announced for December 2020.

sources.title: derestaurantkrant.nl — The Jane
sources.claim: The Jane (Antwerp) closed in 2025 after 11 years.

sources.title: foodandwinegazette.com — Bril/Herman split
sources.claim: Sergio Herman exited The Jane; Nick Bril took full ownership before closure.

sources.title: bestemergingchefs.wordpress.com
sources.claim: The Flemish Foodies are Olly Ceulenaere, Jason Blanckaert, Kobe Desramaults — credited with putting Ghent on the global food map.

sources.title: worldofmouth.app — Privéprivée
sources.claim: Privéprivée is Sergio Herman's private dining room beneath his Antwerp apartment, max 24 guests.

sources.title: lofrestaurant.com
sources.claim: LOF Restaurant is the gastronomic table inside Pillows Grand Boutique Hotel Reylof in Ghent.

sources.title: pillowshotels.com — De Groote
sources.claim: Paul de Groote spent 20 years at three-star Inter Scaldes under Brevet; became chef at LOF in April 2024.

sources.title: gaultmillau.be — Ten to Taste Gent
sources.claim: Vrijmoed tops Gault&Millau's 2026 'Ten to Taste Gent' shortlist at 17.5/20.
